texto: In fifteen hundred years someone will figure out a way to squeeze black juice out of the yellow sand, and that will get everyone very excited. Some people who were rich already will get a lot richer, and some people who were poor will be told that they're richer but will be pretty sure they're not.
explicacao: Citação de Louise Carey sobre temas ligados a reflexao. “In fifteen hundred years someone will figure out a way to squeeze black juice out of the yellow s…” articula reflexao, greed, poor em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como poor e rich.

frase_original: In fifteen hundred years someone will figure out a way to squeeze black juice out of the yellow sand, and that will get everyone very excited. Some people who were rich already will get a lot richer, and some people who were poor will be told that they're richer but will be pretty sure they're not.

texto: A curiosity: my name, Rem, will someday come to mean a line of text in a language spoken only by machines. Specifically, it will mean a line that the machines can safely ignore--one that's only there as a mnemonic, a placeholder, for the people who give the machines their orders. A REM line might say something like "this bit is a self-contained sub loop" or "Steve Perlman in Marketing is a shit." The program as a whole rolls on past and around the REM lines, ignores them completely as it takes its shape, moves through its pre-ordained sequences, unfolds its wonders. My mother named me well.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Louise Carey: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a familia. O trecho “A curiosity: my name, Rem, will someday come to mean a line of text in a language spoken only by …” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que familia, history, ignoring-history pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de ignoring-history e memory.

texto: Sooner or later our souls find their centre of gravity in a hot, salt-tasting kiss and a trembling touch. Trembling is a good sign: it means you're open to a world that knows you're coming.
explicacao: Citação de Louise Carey sobre temas ligados a reflexao. “Sooner or later our souls find their centre of gravity in a hot, salt-tasting kiss and a tremblin…” articula reflexao, finding-yourself, self-awareness em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como self-awareness e sex-analogy.

texto: It's the story of the City of Women; of how it came to be, how it flourished, and how it was destroyed by a reckless and irrevocable act of mercy.
